message:
feat(grpc): add HealthWatcher, grpc-timeout parsing, and default server timeout

Health service enhancements:
- Add a monotonic version counter (AtomicU64) to HealthService that is
  bumped on every status mutation (set_status, set_serving, clear,
  clear_status). This enables efficient change detection without polling
  the full status map.
- Add HealthWatcher type implementing the polling-based Watch semantic
  from the gRPC Health Checking Protocol. Watchers capture the current
  version at construction; subsequent changed() calls detect whether any
  status mutation has occurred since the last check.
- Export HealthWatcher from the grpc module public API.
- Drop RwLock guards before bumping the version counter to avoid holding
  the lock during the atomic store.

Server timeout support:
- Add parse_grpc_timeout() to decode the gRPC timeout header format
  (value + unit suffix: H/M/S/m/u/n) into std::time::Duration. Handles
  overflow via checked_mul.
- Add format_grpc_timeout() to encode a Duration as millisecond-unit
  gRPC timeout header value.
- Add default_timeout field to ServerConfig and corresponding
  default_timeout() builder method, allowing servers to enforce a
  deadline when clients omit the grpc-timeout header.
- Extend CallContext with deadline management: from_metadata() extracts
  the grpc-timeout header and computes an Instant deadline, with
  fallback to the server's default_timeout. Add is_expired(),
  remaining(), and deadline() accessors.
- Export parse_grpc_timeout and format_grpc_timeout from the grpc module.

Add tests/grpc_production_features.rs (865 lines) covering:
- HealthWatcher change detection and per-service status tracking
- grpc-timeout header parsing for all unit suffixes, edge cases, and
  malformed inputs
- format_grpc_timeout round-trip fidelity
- CallContext deadline extraction, expiry, and default timeout fallback
- ServerBuilder configuration composition

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
